title: Slow Mode for Groups (Per-User Message Rate Limiting)
description: Group admins can enforce a per-user cooldown between successive messages in a group. After sending a message, a user must wait the configured interval before sending another. Admins and bots are exempt. Protects against flooding and spam in public/large communities.

server_architecture.enforcement_layer: API Gateway (not application server) — ensures zero bypass possible via direct DB writes

cooldown_tracking.store: Redis — chosen for sub-millisecond TTL precision and atomic SETNX semantics

on_send_attempt: 2. IF last_ts EXISTS AND now() - last_ts < interval → reject with 429, include Retry-After header
3. ELSE: SET key=now() EX=interval+5 NX (atomic — prevents race condition between concurrent requests)
algorithm.race_condition_analysis: Two concurrent sends from same user: SETNX guarantees only one wins. Loser gets 429. No double-write to messages table.

server_architecture.exemption_check: Fetched from PostgreSQL conversations_members cache (1s TTL in Redis) to avoid hot DB reads

body.slow_mode_interval: integer (must be one of allowed values)

configure.auth: JWT with admin or owner role in group
configure.idempotency: Last-write-wins on group_slow_mode_config; concurrent admin changes → no deadlock risk (single-row upsert)

failure_handling.redis_unavailable: Fail-open: allow message send, emit metric alert. Fail-closed would degrade UX globally for transient Redis outage — documented trade-off.
failure_handling.clock_skew: Server-side timestamps only; client clock NOT trusted. interval TTL extended by +5s to absorb NTP jitter.

ui.composer_state: While cooldown active: send button disabled, timer countdown visible ('Next message in 42s'). Timer driven by server-provided next_allowed_at, not local clock.
ui.admin_settings: Group Settings → Slow Mode → picker with predefined intervals (Off / 10s / 30s / 1m / 5m / 15m / 1h)

metrics: counter: slowmode.rejected{group_id} — track which groups generate most rejections

security.privilege_escalation: Client cannot self-grant admin exemption. Server validates role on every request from conversation_members_cache.
security.config_tampering: Slow mode config changes require admin JWT — RLS policy enforces admin-only writes to group_slow_mode_config.
